narrative.headline: 2018 Subaru Forester: visibility/wiper complaints lead, with airbag warnings close behind
narrative.summary: Across 485 owner complaints to NHTSA, visibility/wiper is the most-reported area at 135 reports (28%), followed by air bags at 89 reports (18%) and the electrical system at 68 reports (14%). Most categories report no crashes, fires, or injuries, but vehicle speed control stands out with 13 crashes and the engine category logs 6 crashes, 4 injuries, and the only fire in this data.

issues.sample: I PURCHASED THIS CAR 7/31/18. ON 12/21/18 (ODOMETER 3,427 MILES), THE FIRST MORNING WITH A HARD FROST, I FOUND THAT MY 2018 SUBARU FORESTER (STATIONARY AND PARKED IN MY DRIVEWAY) HAD DEVELOPED A CRACK OVERNIGHT FROM THE LOWER LEFT CORNER OF THE DRIVER'S SIDE UPWARD. (NO CRACK THE
issues.title: Visibility/Wiper
issues.blurb: Most-reported area: 135 complaints (28%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. One owner describes a windshield that cracked overnight on its own while the car sat parked in cold weather.

issues.sample: FOR 2 MONTHS SINCE MAY 19, 2018, THE AIRBAG WARNING SENSOR LIGHT GOES ON MY DASHBOARD WHILE DRIVING AND THE PASSENGER AIRBAG 'OFF' WARNING LIGHT ALSO GOES ON ' SEE ATTACHED PHOTO.  THIS OCCURRENCE HAPPENS ALMOST EVERY TIME I DRIVE MY VEHICLE.  I BROUGHT THE VEHICLE TO THE DEALER
issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: 89 complaints (18%), including 2 crashes and 3 injuries. Owners describe the airbag warning light and passenger airbag 'OFF' light coming on while driving.

issues.sample: TL* THE CONTACT OWNS A 2018 SUBARU FORESTER. WHILE DRIVING APPROXIMATELY 80 MPH, THE VEHICLE SUDDENLY LOST ENGINE POWER. THE VEHICLE WAS COASTED TO AN EXIT RAMP. THE CONTACT STATED THAT THE ENGINE WAS STILL IDLING EXTREMELY LOW AND THE VEHICLE WAS TURNED OFF AND RESTARTED. RIMROC
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 68 complaints (14%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. One owner describes the vehicle suddenly losing engine power while driving at highway speed.

issues.sample: 2018 SUBARU FORESTER 2.5I AUTOMATIC CVT: DEFECTIVE LOW GEAR - SAFETY ISSUE. 21 DAYS AFTER PURCHASE TOOK IT TO DURANGO COLORADO MOUNTAINS, PUT IT IN LOW GEAR BEFORE DESCENDING AND THE LOW GEAR NEVER KICKED IN TO SLOW THE VEHICLE DOWN. STOPPED MULTIPLE TIMES TO RE-ATTEMPT AND LOW G
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 31 complaints (6%), and the most serious category here: 6 crashes, 4 injuries, and the only fire (1) in this data. One owner describes the low gear failing to slow the vehicle on a mountain descent.

issues.sample: WHEN ATTEMPTING TO ACCELERATE TO MAKE A LEFT-HAND TURN THE CAR STOPPED - THE ENGINE WOULD NOT GET ABOVE IDLE, EVEN WHEN THE ACCELERATOR WAS PRESSED TO THE FLOOR.  THE CAR STOPPED IN ONCOMING TRAFFIC.  IT DID ACCELERATE AFTER COMPLETELY REMOVING PRESSURE FROM THE PEDAL AND PUSHING
issues.title: Vehicle Speed Control
issues.blurb: 30 complaints (6%), but with the most crashes of any category here: 13 crashes and 1 injury. Owners describe the car failing to accelerate above idle when pressing the gas, sometimes stalling in traffic.
